_comment: Deterministic time formulas per process. Constants sourced from Machinery's Handbook and vendor spec sheets.
_calibration: Tune against real Schneider PO data when available.

LASER_CUT.description: Time based on cut perimeter and pierce count per material+thickness

setup_formula._note: 5 min base (program load) + 1.5 min per pierce for path verification. Caps at 15 min.

WATERJET.description: Slower than laser; handles thick plate and hard materials

setup_formula._note: Waterjet setup slightly longer — abrasive setup, head check.

TUBE_LASER.description: Linear cutting length along tube profile

setup_formula._note: Tube requires chuck setup and rotation calibration — longer base than flat laser.

PRESS_BRAKE.description: Time per bend scales with both bend count and bend chord length. Longer bends require more positioning, back-gauge adjustment, and angle checking across the full length.

setup_formula._note: 5 min base for tool load + 2 min per additional bend for angle adjustment/tryout.

PRESS_BRAKE._calibration: time_per_in_hr: 0.002 = ~7 sec/inch of bend length for positioning/handling. Tune against real brake tickets.

TAP.description: Time per tapped hole. Assumes standard machine tapping.

setup_formula._note: Fixed 2 min — tap chuck load. Count doesn't change setup.

DRILL.description: Drill press time per hole. Use for simple through/blind holes ≤ 0.5" dia with no tight tolerance callout. Cheaper than CNC mill.

setup_formula._note: Fixed 5 min — drill press setup is minimal.

CNC_MILL.description: Time per milled feature (hole, pocket, slot, contour op). Setup covers program load and fixturing.

setup_formula._note: 20 min base (program load + fixture) + 2 min per feature. CNC setup is non-trivial.

LATHE.description: Time per turned feature. Setup covers chuck and tool setup.

setup_formula._note: 15 min base for chuck and tool selection.

WELD_TIG.description: TIG weld time per inch of weld bead. cut_length_in = total weld bead length in inches.

setup_formula._note: 5 min base fixturing + 1 min per weld joint.

PAINT.description: Spray paint (machine color or specified color). Cost scales continuously with blank area as a proxy for painted surface area.

setup_formula._note: Fixed — rack and hang time per part.

POWDER_COAT.description: Electrostatic powder coat. Heavier per-area cost than paint — thicker coat, oven cure.

setup_formula._note: Fixed — rack and hang time per part.

POWDER_COAT._calibration: 0.00040hr/sq_in. Tune against real powder coat invoices.

ANODIZE.description: Aluminum only. Tank time scales with surface area.

setup_formula._note: Fixed — rack and hang time per part.

ANODIZE._calibration: 0.00060hr/sq_in. Tank time is the dominant cost driver.

WELD_MIG.description: MIG weld time per inch of weld bead. Faster than TIG — 2.5x feed rate. Carbon steel only.

setup_formula._note: 3 min base fixturing + 0.6 min per weld joint. MIG setup faster than TIG.

WELD_MIG._calibration: 0.008hr/in = ~29 sec/in. MIG is ~2.5x faster than TIG (0.020hr/in). Tune against real MIG tickets.

BLAST.description: Abrasive or glass-bead blast for surface prep before coating. Time scales with blank area.

setup_formula._note: Fixed — cabinet setup and part hang.

BLAST._calibration: 0.00030hr/sq_in. Faster than paint; thin parts blast quickly.

ZINC_PLATE.description: Zinc electroplating for corrosion protection. Tank soak time scales with surface area.

setup_formula._note: Fixed — rack, degrease, activate, rinse steps before tank.

ZINC_PLATE._calibration: 0.00100hr/sq_in = longer tank soak than anodize. Tune against plating invoices.

CHROMATE.description: Chromate conversion coating on aluminum (MIL-DTL-5541 Type I or II). Fixed time — fast chemical dip regardless of part size at Schneider part scale.

setup_formula._note: Fixed — deox, rinse, dip, rinse, dry. Quantity = 1 per part.

CHROMATE._calibration: 0.25hr fixed is conservative for small aluminum parts. Tune if large panels.

LASER_MARK.description: Laser marking or engraving (part number, logo, serial). Same rate per mark as DRILL.

setup_formula._note: Fixed 3 min — fixture and program load. Mark time itself is fast.

LASER_MARK._calibration: 0.017hr/mark = same as drill press. Tune if marks are complex vector fills.
